worlds.name: Pixar 3D Feature
worlds.formula: Premium 3D animated feature look (Pixar/DreamWorks tier), material-agnostic
worlds.render: Premium 3D animated feature frame at studio-feature fidelity: appealing rounded forms with clean readable silhouette, subsurface-soft skin and material response, multi-bounce global-illumination lighting with one warm motivated key, gentle depth-of-field, art-directed colour script, emotional staging, polished cinematic composition. Production-grade, never plasticky game-engine, never photoreal. 10-second motion arc: vibrant 3D world establishes → one character action or physics event with expressive weight → warm confident hold.
worlds.motion: weighted squash-and-stretch ease, one expressive anticipation, settle into a confident hold
worlds.best: warm emotional explainers, character-led lessons, broad family appeal
worlds.avoid: uncanny photorealism, flat cel, stiff game-engine CGI

worlds.name: Anime Cel (ufotable-grade)
worlds.formula: Premium Japanese cel animation with 3D-assisted FX and painterly BG
worlds.render: Premium anime cel frame at top-studio (ufotable/Kyoto-tier) fidelity: crisp cel shading with 2-3 clean value steps, expressive hand-drawn line, detailed painted background with atmospheric depth, dramatic rim and bloom, lens-flare accents, 3D-composited effects (water, flame, energy light) layered over 2D character and hand-painted background, single emotive character in mid-shot; golden-hour rim light separating them from the painted background, emotional golden-hour palette. 10-second motion arc: painted world holds → one emotionally charged action or revelation → elemental particle settle and warm hold. IP-safe original design — no copied characters.
worlds.motion: smear-frame impact then ease, effects-animation flourish, held dramatic beat
worlds.best: shonen energy, emotional action explainers, dramatic reveals
worlds.avoid: western cartoon proportions, photoreal, muddy shading

worlds.name: Arcane — Painterly 3D
worlds.formula: Fortiche-idiom painterly 3D: hand-painted texture over 3D form
worlds.render: Painterly 3D feature frame in the Fortiche idiom: hand-painted texture maps over 3D geometry with visible brush-stroke albedo, 2D-animated smoke and energy FX composited onto 3D bodies, one figure at the teal-ember boundary — heavy shadow mass behind, single ember accent isolating the decisive detail, chromatic rim light, deep teal-and-ember palette, heavy art-directed negative-space shadow, expressive non-photoreal skin, graphic ink accents along silhouette edges, gallery-grade gritty mood. 10-second motion arc: oppressive environment establishes → one charged emotional gesture or reveal → dramatic shadow aftermath hold. No photoreal CGI, no flat cel, no clean game-engine look.
worlds.motion: deliberate weighted move, painterly FX flourish, brooding final hold
worlds.best: gritty emotional drama, mature stylised storytelling, hero moments
worlds.avoid: clean photoreal CGI, flat cartoon, glossy plastic

worlds.name: Spider-Verse — Comic Motion
worlds.formula: Into-the-Spider-Verse idiom: comic-book print motion graphics
worlds.render: Graphic comic-motion frame in the Spider-Verse idiom: each frame compositionally complete as a comic panel — motion conveyed through bold ink motion lines, halftone Ben-Day energy bursts and impact freeze poses, visible CMYK mis-registration offset, bold ink outlines, screen-printed colour layer, onomatopoeia and motion-line graphics as design elements, painterly-graphic backgrounds with chromatic aberration, high-energy panel composition. 10-second motion arc: graphic panel-composition intro → one kinetic event with motion-line energy → graphic impact freeze hold. IP-safe original design — print-art-direction quality, not flat vector.
worlds.motion: stepped 2s-on-3s snap, graphic motion-shape trails, crisp pose-to-pose hold
worlds.best: kinetic pop energy, bold youth content, high-impact reveals
worlds.avoid: smooth photoreal interpolation, muted realism, plain flat color

worlds.name: Ghibli — Watercolor 2D
worlds.formula: Hand-drawn 2D with watercolor backgrounds and gentle naturalism
worlds.render: Hand-drawn 2D animation frame in the Ghibli idiom: soft watercolor and gouache painted backgrounds with atmospheric depth, gentle clean character line, naturalistic warm light, one small figure against a vast living landscape — environment in gentle continuous motion (grass bending, clouds drifting, water surface alive), figure in one slow deliberate intentional gesture, tender humane staging, nostalgic pastel-but-rich palette, hand-crafted warmth. 10-second motion arc: living environment establishes fully → figure makes one quiet deliberate gesture → world settles in gentle warmth. No 3D CGI, no harsh cel banding, no digital sheen.
worlds.motion: gentle natural drift, wind and breath, calm settle
worlds.best: calm nature/feeling-led lessons, wonder, slow emotional beats
worlds.avoid: 3D CGI, hard cel, neon digital color

worlds.render: Tactile stop-motion frame at Laika fidelity: real handcrafted miniature sets and puppets, visible fabric/clay/wood material grain and fingerprint imperfection in every still frame, shallow macro depth-of-field, moody practical key light, tangible physical material weight — every frame complete and photographable on its own, no smooth CGI finish. 10-second motion arc: miniature world establishes in full tactile detail → one deliberate physical action completes its full arc with natural weight and follow-through → setting holds as a photographable final frame. worlds.best: crafty tactile explainers, cozy handmade charm, wonder
worlds.avoid: smooth CGI interpolation, flat vector, photoreal live action

worlds.name: MAPPA — Cinematic Dark Anime
worlds.formula: MAPPA-studio high-detail dark cinematic anime with fluid motion and atmosphere
worlds.render: Premium cinematic anime frame at MAPPA-studio fidelity: high-detail character linework with expressive facial microanimation, heavy atmospheric background painting with volumetric haze and urban texture, motion force conveyed through atmospheric light trails, shadow blur and dark aftermath — one decisive motion event per shot, sharp value contrast (deep shadow masses, single bright accent cutting one face plane), desaturated gritty palette with selective color pops on emotional peaks, dynamic diagonal compositions. Dark, adult, technically uncompromising. Single figure at the tension center; key light isolates exactly one face plane from deep shadow. 10-second motion arc: oppressive still atmosphere → one explosive committed event → settling dark atmospheric aftermath hold. IP-safe original design — no copied characters.

worlds.best: dark action series energy, intense emotional confrontation, gritty drama, mature narrative
worlds.avoid: flat cheerful cel, clean Pixar warmth, pastel softness, western cartoon proportions

worlds.render: Clean high-fidelity action anime frame at Bones-studio caliber: precise expressive linework with clean ink outlines, smooth dynamic action choreography with weight and follow-through, clean decisive negative space between figure and background at the action peak — choreography always fully readable at full speed, rich warm shadows (3-value clean shading), detailed mechanical and elemental FX animation with crisp edges, strong emotive character expression, hand-painted background art with textural depth, warm amber-and-blue palette with one saturated accent on the action peak. 10-second motion arc: weight-loaded anticipation hold → one technically perfect motion event with full follow-through → confident settled landing. IP-safe original design — no copied characters.

worlds.best: action-driven character moments, elemental/power FX reveals, dramatic emotional peaks with kinetic energy
worlds.avoid: muddy overlapping linework, photoreal, dark desaturated MAPPA palette, flat vector

worlds.render: Classic grand adventure anime frame in the Toei Animation idiom: bold clean ink outlines with expressive character proportions, highly saturated vibrant color palette (ocean blues, sun golds, bright primaries), one clear hero figure in primary full light, full-body silhouette against a vast open horizon, ocean or sky — figure-to-world scale always explicit and celebrated, dynamic exaggerated expressions and poses, strong graphic silhouette reads, punchy color-hold effects on impact beats, hand-painted cel-era background warmth. 10-second motion arc: vast horizon or open world reveals → hero rises or arrives into frame → triumphant arrival pose and warm color hold. refs.name: Satoshi Kon — Dream Cut Grammar

The edit completes the action from the other side of a reality boundary. Result: the viewer's brain performs the logic of sanity on footage that doesn't have any. The psychological dissolve is invisible on first viewing, uncanny on second. Palette: hyper-real commercial saturation in the dream layer, desaturated domestic realism in the waking layer.

Soft subsurface-scatter on organic materials (skin, fur, food). A single warm motivated key from camera-right, gentle ambient from the sky, soft shadow footprint. The staging is always about felt scale — large when triumphant, small when afraid. Composition tells the emotion before the face confirms it.

refs.name: Blade Runner — Neon Noir Grammar
refs.use: Dystopia, urban loneliness, technology vs humanity, detective noir, sci-fi atmosphere
refs.dna: Neo-noir rain city: deep blue-black void above, vertical rain catching neon (red, amber, blue) as falling curtains of light. Subject lit from above by a single neon source, rendering them as rim-lit silhouette with colored edge. Steam or smoke diffuses the mid-ground. Wet pavement doubles the neon geometry below as a mirror city. No warm key light — only cool or neon. Buildings are unreachably tall. The city is infinite, indifferent, and magnificent.

refs.name: Oppenheimer — Analog Density Grammar
refs.use: Historical weight, science, consequence, genius and destruction, analog authenticity
refs.dna: Nolan's IMAX analog texture: extreme close-up on physical matter — machined metal, glass lens, reactor graphite, human iris — filling the full frame with material information. Desaturated palette: warm amber, iron grey, sepia, bone. Practical fire, arc-welding spark, or nuclear light as the only source. No CGI. Each frame could be a 70mm still from 1945. An extreme close-up on one eye reflects the event. Sound and silence alternate; the visual information IS the score.

refs.name: Dune — Desert Monumental Grammar

refs.dna: Villeneuve's scale-as-awe: one figure registers as a pixel against a sand dune the size of a mountain range, or a sandworm fin rising from the desert floor like a continent. Horizon line impossibly high or low — no comfortable altitude. Bleached sky meeting sand at geometric angles. Palette: pale warm gold, cool blue shadow in dune valleys, overexposed sky. Zero motion blur — the universe is still and the human is small. Silence implied. The world is a cathedral that does not know you.

refs.name: John Wick — Neon Precision Grammar
refs.use: Precision action, gun-fu choreography, nightclub/hotel environments, tactical elegance
refs.dna: Tactical choreography lit by practicals: a figure in black against high-contrast nightclub, hotel corridor, or continental rooftop. One neon key from above in a primary hue (electric blue, blood red, amber gold). Long shot holds the full body — no coverage cuts mid-sequence, one spatial understanding. Hard specular on wet floor. The camera is at rest while violence is in motion — the framing is architectural, not kinetic. Each action is legible, not blurred.

refs.name: Wes Anderson — Symmetry Grammar
refs.use: Deadpan comedy, nostalgia, obsessive order, coming-of-age, hotel/train/ship environments
refs.dna: Perfect bilateral symmetry about the vertical center line — camera level with subject eyeline, mounted on a horizontal slider, frontal. Every prop, person, and color swatch placed on a graphic grid. Color palette: one dominant pastel hue, one complementary accent, cream or white as the neutral field. Flat overhead fill with no motivated shadow — the world has no sun, only a production designer. Deadpan centered figures whose comedy arrives from perfect staging, never from expression. Each frame feels like a diorama under museum glass.

refs.name: Fincher — Controlled Green Noir
refs.use: Psychological thriller, corporate menace, hacker culture, control, obsession, digital darkness
refs.dna: Fincher's digital-age control: a desaturated teal-green color grade lowering ambient luminance, every surface rendered in its darkest photographic truth — black leather, wet concrete, server racks. Sharp focus across the full field with no creative bokeh. Subjects lit from above with minimal fill, creating strong under-eye shadow that reads as intelligence or danger. The camera moves with mechanical precision: exact rack, exact pan speed. Nothing is accidental. The world has been color-corrected to match the psychological state.

refs.name: Kubrick — One-Point Dread Grammar
refs.use: Institutional settings, authority, controlled dread, geometry as power, symmetry as threat
refs.dna: One-point perspective down the spine of a corridor, highway, or stairwell — every parallel line collapsing to a single vanishing point dead center. Locked camera, no handheld, no rack focus. Slow imperceptible push taking ten seconds to register. Subjects placed symmetrically flanking the recession spine, never blocking the vanishing point. Predominantly cool blue-grey or institutional green tonality, one warm anomaly (a lamp, a window). Dread emerges from geometric perfection, not from monster or violence. The frame feels like architecture dreamed it.

refs.name: Deakins — Naturalist Light Grammar
refs.use: Realism, character drama, prison/rural/domestic settings, craft invisibility, human truth
refs.dna: Deakins' invisible cinematography: motivated practical light only — one window, one lamp, one fire. No fill from off-screen sources unless motivated. Color temperature matches what the scene physically has: dawn is blue-grey, noon is harsh white, interior night is tungsten amber. Faces fully exposed but with real shadow depth. No cinematic grade — the image looks like reality but it is the best possible reality. The photography serves the actor, not the director's signature.

refs.name: Lubezki — Natural Long-Take Grammar
refs.use: Immersion, war sequences, magical realism, nature, consciousness-of-place, unbroken time
refs.dna: Lubezki's unbroken world: a single continuous take beginning inside a space and ending outside it — through windows, across open fields, through doorways — the camera flowing like a living thing at human shoulder height. Natural light only, meaning the image changes as clouds pass. No fixed focal length; the depth of field breathes with the camera's distance to subject. The world is real and the take proves it by never cutting away from it.

refs.name: Apple — Object Worship Grammar
refs.use: Product reveal, engineering minimalism, brand identity, desire-object staging, premium consumer
refs.dna: Apple's product theology: a single device in absolute void — one edge-light revealing the object geometry with surgical precision, a single perfect specular highlight traveling the curved surface from one edge to the other. Shadow footprint is clean and deliberate. No context, no hand, no user — the product is the world. The silence of the frame communicates that nothing else exists. Palette: pure white void or deep absolute black void. Product finish is the only texture.

Hard directional key light from above creating a motion-implied shadow streak on the ground below. The body is a piece of kinetic sculpture. Palette: skin tone against white, black, or brand-specific color field. Minimal environment — one surface, one direction.

Background: absolute void. The watch case edge catches light as a thin luminous arc. No color — all silver, black, and the blue of blued steel.

refs.dna: Rolex's object permanence: the Oyster case on a perfectly neutral surface, a single large-format key light from above-left at 45 degrees, creating one precise specular highlight that travels the case curve from bezel to lug. The date magnifier lens catches its own miniature world-in-reflection. Palette: warm champagne gold, cool rhodium silver, deep blue or black dial. The watch does not move. It does not need to — it communicates through absolute stillness.

refs.name: Red Beverage Refresh Grammar
refs.use: Summer refreshment, appetite desire, classic brand warmth, beverage close-up staging
refs.dna: Classic Coke desire staging: a glass bottle with condensation drops running down the surface at golden backlight — the light passes through the amber liquid and the glass neck simultaneously, creating a warm concentrated glow at the bottle's center. The red label catches the backlight as a saturated warm band. A hand closes around the bottle creating a grip-impression in the condensation. Palette: Coca-Cola red, warm amber-gold light, white condensation, neutral warm background.

refs.name: Warm Café Lifestyle Grammar
refs.use: Café warmth, comfort, morning ritual, creative space, social connection, slow beverage moments
refs.dna: Third-place comfort staging: a ceramic latte-art cup at a wood-grain table, steam curling from the surface in one continuous upward S-curve. Key light from above-left, warm tungsten quality, catching the coffee surface and creating a soft shadow footprint. Bokeh background of café practicals (amber orbs, out-of-focus wooden shelves). The cup is held by hands — not studio floating. Palette: dark espresso brown, steamed-milk white, warm café amber, natural wood grain.

refs.name: Pop Fast-Food Grammar
refs.use: Food appetite, fast-food scale, burger close-up, QSR commercial grammar, pop-food energy
refs.dna: Appetite staging at golden hour: a sesame-seed bun slightly above eye level, shot with a 50mm at f/2 — the sesame seeds sharp, the lettuce edge soft, the cheese pull still in motion below. Warm key light from above creating specular on the bun's top surface. Background: clean warm yellow-red chromatic suggestion, no specific set. The color temperature is 3200K, pushed warmer in grade. Every element exists to make you hungry in 0.5 seconds.

refs.name: Arcane × Clay Hybrid
refs.use: Clay material meets prestige painterly animation, tactile fantasy world, textured dark drama
refs.avoid: Actual Arcane/League IP, dark/violent tone — keep educational warmth
refs.dna: Fortiche's painterly brush on clay-like material surfaces: the Arcane visual grammar of oil-paint texture strokes applied to characters rendered with the physical depth of stop-motion clay or felt. Every surface has both the painted quality of Zaun's industrial grime AND the tactile physical reality of a handmade object you could hold. Lighting emphasizes depth of material — key light creates shadow that reveals the clay thickness, not just the painted surface.

refs.name: Spider-Verse × Paper Hybrid
refs.use: Comic book physics, paper world construction, Miles-verse aesthetic, graphic story identity
refs.avoid: Actual Marvel IP, violent Spider-Man content
refs.dna: Spider-Verse paper world: environments constructed from visible layers of cut paper — background a painted landscape, midground a separate paper cutout layer, character on a top paper-flat cutout layer. The parallax of these layers is visible in camera movement. Comic-book halftone dots on the background layer. Characters cast paper-shadow onto the layer behind them. The world IS a comic book, assembled from its physical components.

refs.name: Ghibli × Felt Hybrid
refs.use: Handmade warmth, Ghibli characters, craft-world aesthetic, childhood-toy atmosphere
refs.dna: Studio Ghibli's textile warmth applied to felt-craft material: Ghibli's characteristic organic line and natural color palette, but every surface reads as handmade felt — animals have the fuzzy texture of embroidered wool, environments have the slightly rough surface of needle-felted landscape. The imperfection of craft IS the warmth of the world. Key light is gentle and diffused — the kind that shows felt texture without hard shadow.

refs.name: Anime × Chalk Hybrid
refs.use: Educational content in anime style, classroom teaching moments, subject concept visualization
refs.avoid: Copying specific anime character chalk drawings, too much violence energy
refs.dna: Anime visual language on blackboard chalk surface: dramatic shonen or shojo staging with the specific texture of chalk on dark board — linework is slightly rough at the edges, fills have the slight skip of chalk on texture, highlights are white chalk marks. The blackboard is the canvas; the anime grammar is the content. Bold chalk outlines, flat chalk-pigment color fills, smudge shadows. The whole frame could be erased with one hand.

refs.name: Pixar × Watercolor Hybrid
refs.use: Wonder-filled science, nature cycles, emotional journeys for children, biology and ecology
refs.avoid: Copying Pixar IP characters, photo-realistic rendering — keep painterly and warm
refs.dna: Pixar soft emotional key light motivating wet-on-wet watercolor wash backgrounds — visible paper grain on flat surfaces, color fields bleed warmly at edges where two washes meet, the dominant concept object rests on a patch of reserved white paper, child-wonder scale staging, diffused fill with no hard shadow edges

refs.name: Stop-Motion × Wood Hybrid
refs.use: Mechanical processes, how-things-work, construction sequences, physics and engineering for children
refs.avoid: Digital smooth animation feel — the handmade stutter and wood grain must be visible
refs.dna: Laika-grade stop-motion puppet weight built from carved and turned wood — each pivot joint clearly shows the grain and varnish catching the key light, camera close enough to see the wood texture and jointed mechanism, deliberate one-frame-at-a-time settle after each move, slight motion blur on action peak, stable final frame with the mechanism fully extended

refs.name: Kurzgesagt × Sand Hybrid
refs.use: System cycles, cosmic scale, water/food/energy cycles, abstract process explanations
refs.avoid: Rough or unclear sand forms — sand silhouettes must read as clean geometric Kurzgesagt shapes
refs.dna: Kurzgesagt flat-vector diagram logic rebuilt as backlit sand-art — warm amber lightbox glow illuminates grain silhouettes from below, sand flows and resettles as the concept transforms between states, isometric bird's-eye vantage locked overhead, smooth grain drift on the moving element only, stilled sand pool as the final stable hold

refs.name: Whiteboard × Ink-Brush Hybrid
refs.use: Step-by-step processes, cause-and-effect chains, how-to sequences, lesson walkthroughs
refs.avoid: Messy or illegible strokes — every ink line must read clearly against the white surface
refs.dna: RSA-Animate whiteboard draw-on grammar with East Asian sumi-e ink-brush quality — single-stroke calligraphic weight as the drawing hand traces each concept element in sequence, white negative space dominates the frame, selective accent-color marker wash floods behind the key concept node, camera wide and locked as the diagram builds from left to right across the board

refs.name: Ghibli × Watercolor Hybrid
refs.use: Seasons, environment, cultural traditions, gentle emotional arcs, early childhood empathy content
refs.avoid: Copying Ghibli IP characters or specific Ghibli scenes
refs.dna: Ghibli diffused organic side-window key light applied to academic watercolor technique — wet-on-wet wash bleeding freely at sky and horizon edges, visible granulation in shadow fields, white paper highlights preserved on the primary concept subject, no hard outline on any edge, all boundaries softly bleeding into neighboring wash fields, supremely gentle temperature bias

refs.name: Anime × Wax-Crayon Hybrid
refs.use: Lively and bold lesson starters, exciting discovery moments, high-energy concept introductions for children
refs.avoid: Too much detail — wax crayon honesty requires bold, simple forms only
refs.dna: bold-outline anime energy expressed entirely through wax crayon drawing — every line a child hand-drawn crayon mark, colour blocked in thick overlapping strokes, emotional composition with large expressive face and oversized hands, joyful saturated palette on cream paper grain

refs.name: Three-Point Beauty Studio
refs.use: 50-85mm; soft key + fill + hair/rim; controlled falloff; seamless or built set
refs.avoid: on-camera flash, muddy shadows, plastic skin
refs.dna: classic three-point product separation — hard key from 45-degree above-front sculpting form, soft fill from opposite side reducing shadow ratio, rimlight behind separating subject edge from background, no ambient spill, total control over every shadow and highlight surface

refs.name: Window-Light Testimonial
refs.use: 35-50mm at eye height; soft north-window key camera-left; gentle negative fill; real room depth
refs.avoid: beauty smoothing, fake bokeh, fake smile
refs.dna: honest window portrait light — single diffused north-facing window as sole source, soft wrap on skin texture revealing authentic pore detail, gentle fall-off to shadow side, off-camera documentary calm, working-distance 85mm compression preserving facial spatial truth

refs.name: Product Macro Tabletop
refs.use: 90-100mm macro; single hard key + black/white flags; focus-stack feel; reflection control
refs.avoid: plastic CGI sheen, warped logo, extra props
refs.dna: precision tabletop product geometry — overhead or 45-degree key controlled to within one stop, negative space left intentionally to let product edge breathe, surface reflection choreographed to confirm product silhouette, edge highlights from secondary small kicker confirming geometry precision

refs.name: Handheld Verite Doc
refs.use: 35mm handheld micro-drift; available light; follow real action; no staging
refs.avoid: stabilized gloss, stock smiling, staged crowd
refs.dna: verite documentary present-tense witness — imperfect motivated operator position (camera finds subject, subject does not find camera), micro-reframing only when action forces it, available light from room practicals, grain and motion-blur acceptable if honest to the moment

refs.name: Golden-Hour Automotive
refs.use: 24-35mm low; long warm sun rim; tracking + parallax; reflection pass on bodywork
refs.avoid: HDR halo, fake lens flare, CGI gloss
refs.dna: golden-hour automotive sculpture — 5-degree low sun raking across vehicle surface at three-quarter angle, long cast shadow confirming wheel contact, metal bodywork catching warm orange-gold specular across body-line sculpture, controlled lens flare from sun edge at frame corner

refs.name: High-Key Fashion Studio
refs.use: 85-105mm; bright even key; seamless white; crisp wardrobe weave
refs.dna: editorial high-key studio clarity — large-format wrap source creating near-zero shadow ratio across skin or product, white or light-grey seamless ground, graphic pose reads as clean silhouette against bright ground, restraint: no rim accent that would undermine clean high-key logic

refs.name: Low-Key Chiaroscuro Portrait
refs.use: 85mm; single hard key + deep falloff; Rembrandt cheek; negative fill
refs.avoid: flat fill, crushed black, beauty smoothing
refs.dna: moody low-key sculpted shadow — single hard key carving exactly one face or product plane from deep shadow, fill ratio near-zero (fill stops down 2-3 stops), background absorbs to near-black, one clean highlight as the emotional center of the composition

refs.name: Practical Neon Night Street
refs.use: 35mm; practical sign sources; wet-asphalt reflection; motivated colour pools
refs.avoid: uniform neon wash, CGI haze, flat fog
refs.dna: night city neon practical density — real neon tube or LED-sign practicals as sole sources, magenta/cyan/amber pool mixing in wet street surface reflection, no artificial key fill supplementing the natural neon logic, coloured shadows confirming layered depth, working distance 35mm for urban scale

refs.name: Overcast Soft Daylight
refs.use: 35-50mm; big soft sky source; low contrast; true colour
refs.avoid: flat boredom without a contrast accent
refs.dna: overcast natural truth — large-format diffused overhead from real sky or industrial window, zero hard shadow edge, shadow-to-highlight ratio close to 1:2 creating product and skin truth without glamour deception, honest tonal range confirming authentic real-world light quality

refs.name: Hard Sun Desert Commercial
refs.use: 24mm; hard low/overhead sun; deep crisp shadows; restrained heat shimmer
refs.dna: grand desert scale raking texture — low sun at five-degree horizon angle raking across vast sand or stone surface at extreme dramatic angle, bold cast shadows at maximum length and contrast, aerial wide-angle establishing scale against tiny figure, hard bright specular on rock crystal or sand grain confirming mid-day hardness

refs.name: Tungsten Interview Warmth
refs.use: 50mm; warm practical key ~3200K; soft fill; practicals as background bokeh
refs.avoid: orange cast overload, fake bokeh balls
refs.dna: intimate tungsten practical warmth — 2700-3200K desk lamp or practicals as sole source, warm amber spill on skin and wall surface with honest soft fall-off, interior depth confirmed by cooler ambient (window or corridor), cinema-vérité credibility through real light logic

refs.name: Anamorphic Wide Landscape
refs.use: wide anamorphic; horizon discipline; oval bokeh; slow parallax
refs.dna: anamorphic grand scale through restraint — 1.33-1.5x squeeze characteristic oval bokeh and horizontal lens flare, widescreen composition (2.39:1 discipline), clean horizon line dividing land/sky at golden ratio, haze layer confirming depth, one strong graphic element at frame

refs.name: Shonen Adventure Narrative Grammar
refs.use: Adventure beginnings, world reveals, character introduction, journey start, inspiration
refs.avoid: protected characters, pirate logos, exact ship/world copy
refs.dna: The establishing shot of a shonen adventure: a wide frame showing a vast world beyond the protagonist's starting point — a road leading to distant mountains, a river flowing to the sea, a horizon with something massive and unknown at its edge. The protagonist is in the lower-left, looking toward the right (the direction of narrative progress). Warm light. Big sky. The world is larger than this person, and that is the invitation. No villain visible. No obstacle named. Only direction.

refs.name: Soul Pressure Reveal Grammar
refs.use: Power reveals, authority hierarchies, spiritual pressure, boss introductions, transcendence moments
refs.avoid: direct character, sword, uniform, logo or world copy
refs.dna: Spiritual hierarchy made physical: a figure radiating an aura so powerful that the environment responds — other figures are forced to their knees or pushed backward, the ground cracks beneath the source, the air distorts and darkens around the radius of influence. The aura has a specific color tied to its nature. The powerful figure is perfectly still at center; everything else deforms around that stillness. Scale of aura = scale of power.

refs.name: Hero Academia-like Burst Energy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Superhero combat, sports matches, peak effort, decisive moment, 'Plus Ultra' emotional beats
refs.avoid: protected hero costumes, emblems, school logos, exact character likeness or frame copy
refs.dna: Bones Studio's hero animation: a figure in mid-air at peak jump, SMASH debris radiating outward from the impact point as a perfect sphere of concrete chunks and dust. Clean cel-shading on the hero — no painterly texture, the lines are ink-bold and the fills are flat. Background temporarily replaces environment with a speed-blur radial vignette at the peak frame, then snaps back to environment in the aftermath. Hero color is always the warmest element in the frame.

refs.name: Hunter-like Tactical Aura Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Strategy, rules-based magic, chess-like tension, martial arts theory, internal calculation
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, license-card props, exact ability designs or frame copy
refs.dna: Madhouse's intellectual action: two figures at rest in an open space — the combat is happening in their minds, visible to us as floating diagrams, glowing nen-aura shapes (spheres, lines, restricted zones), and text that appears in the frame like a rulebook the characters are consulting in real time. The actual physical action is slow, deliberate, minimal. Palette varies by nen type: blue for enhancement, yellow for manipulation, red for emission, purple for conjuration.

refs.name: Vinland-like North Epic Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Norse history, the Viking age, war's cost, father-son conflict, cold-epic atmosphere
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, exact ship ornament, sigils, frame copy
refs.dna: WIT Studio/MAPPA's historical weight: a longship on a grey northern sea under a sky with no visible sun — diffused overcast light flattening everything to the same muted value. The ship is small against the sea. The sea is violent but the frame is still — a very long lens compressing the distance between ship and horizon. Palette: sea-grey, iron-blue, snow-white, rust-blood, the ochre of old wood. Violence has consequence here — bodies remain in frame.

refs.name: Mob-like Psychic Wave Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Psychic power, repressed emotion, coming-of-age, comedy-drama, peak sakuga moments
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, exact aura designs, logos, frame copy
refs.dna: Mob Psycho's psychic waterfall: one ordinary-looking figure surrounded by an eruption of psychic energy rendered as animated paint strokes, hand-drawn speed lines, and abstract shape-language — the animation goes off-model intentionally at the psychic peak, using every technique simultaneously (watercolor wash, ink splatter, pixel burst, 3D smear). The contrast between Mob's blank expression and the surrounding visual explosion IS the joke and the drama simultaneously.

refs.name: Berserk-like Dark Engraving Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Dark medieval fantasy, extreme violence with consequence, fate vs will, traumatic survival
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, brand marks, exact armor/sword design, frame copy
refs.dna: Kentaro Miura's crosshatch darkness: extreme close-up on a hand gripping a sword handle, the hatching lines so dense they create a third value between the ink-black shadow and the paper-white highlight. Atmosphere rendered as scratched sky texture. The violence is physical — armor dents, flesh tears with anatomical specificity. Palette: ink black, parchment off-white, blood rust. The beauty of the draftsmanship exists in direct tension with what it depicts.

refs.name: Monster-like Quiet Dread Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Psychological thriller, serial killer, European realism, clinical dread, mundane horror
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, real-institution logos, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Naoki Urasawa's psychological realism: a figure seated alone at a café table in a European city — the image is documentary-quiet. Nothing violent is happening. The dread comes from the extreme normalcy of the frame: correctly placed cups, correctly lit windows, correctly dressed extras. The monster moves through the world in civilian clothes and the camera knows it, even when it doesn't show it. Palette: realistic German or Czech urban color, no stylization.

refs.name: JoJo-like Pose Graphic Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Fashion aesthetics, dramatic reveals, stand battles, Italian-Renaissance-meets-pop-art
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, stand designs, logos, exact panel copy
refs.dna: Araki's fashion violence: a figure frozen in an impossible pose that no biomechanics permits — weight distributed on two fingers, limbs at angles that suggest sculpture rather than action. The pose is held long enough to be read as an intention, not a freeze-frame. Screen-print color overlays in the background (pinks, purples, neons). Strong bold ink outline. Palette is high-contrast and deliberately garish: magenta, lime, cobalt, orange. The body IS the composition.

refs.name: One-Punch-like Contrast Comedy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Comedy-action, absurdism, power fantasy, contrast of styles, existential boredom
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, hero-association marks, exact frame copy
refs.dna: ONE's contrast logic: a figure drawn with extreme minimalism (simple circle face, flat fills, kindergarten proportions) placed into a background rendered with extreme photorealistic detail — architectural perspective, volumetric smoke, environmental destruction at movie-quality. The comedy and power come from the same source: the protagonist is too simple for the world he is in. Fist connects: a single Bezier curve. Explosion: forty layers of particle simulation.

refs.name: Violet-like Light Elegance Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Emotional restraint, letter-writing, memory, grief, light as feeling, quiet drama
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, uniform/brooch design, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Kyoto Animation's light poetry: extreme close-up on a girl writing at a window, late-afternoon light entering from the upper-left at 45 degrees, passing through white curtains that bloom and diffuse the source. The light lands on her hands, the paper, her cheek. Everything not in the light path is in a warm blue-grey shadow. No drama in the content — the drama is entirely in the quality of light. Palette: white-cream, golden afternoon, cool blue-grey interior shadow.

refs.name: Haikyuu-like Sport Momentum Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Sports, peak athletic moments, team energy, jump serves, spike animation peaks
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, team crests, exact frame copy
refs.dna: Production I.G.'s sports sakuga: a volleyball leaving a player's wrists — the ball is a white-blur motion arc, the wrist snap is a smear of multiple arm positions in one frame. Background: pure speed lines radiating from the point of contact, bleaching everything else from the frame for 3 frames only, then snapping back to the full gymnasium. Palette contrast at the hit moment: the ball is white against a briefly monochrome world.

refs.name: Vagabond-like Ink Brush Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Samurai history, philosophical combat, manga-as-painting, sumi-e brush grammar, stillness
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, historical-figure portraits as identity, exact panel copy
refs.dna: Inoue Takehiko's sumi-e action: a katana swing rendered as a single continuous brushstroke — ink thicker at the origin, thinning to a hair-line at the follow-through, leaving white paper visible within the stroke's body. No grey tones, only ink black and paper white with occasional wash. The figure at the center is the only controlled element; the background is ink-splatter and void. The bamboo grove is five vertical strokes and a suggestion.

refs.name: Abyss-like Vertical Wonder Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Underground exploration, vertical world, scale wonder, dark wonder, mysterious ecology
refs.avoid: protected character likeness, creature copies, relic designs, frame copy
refs.dna: Kinema Citrus's vertical extremity: a shot looking directly down into the Abyss — the rope bridge is a thin line at the top, the layers of the cave system descend with atmospheric perspective converting the lower depths to darkness. The ecology changes at each visible layer: green forest, exposed rock, bioluminescent blue, absolute black. A tiny figure on the rope bridge establishes the scale as overwhelming. Palette: tropical canopy green, ochre stone, bioluminescent teal, absolute deep-cave black.

refs.name: Nolan-like IMAX Practical Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Practical spectacle, real-world physics, war, space, historical drama, temporal confusion
refs.avoid: director name-dropping in output, studio logos, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Nolan's practical IMAX: a 70mm film frame of a real physical event — bomber over a real city, a figure in a practical spacesuit against a real IMAX sky backdrop, an analog detonation in a real desert. The grain of the celluloid is visible. Desaturated warm-brown palette with selective blue-grey for scale. No CGI tells — every edge has the physics of the real world. Time may be non-linear but the photography never knows it.

refs.name: Spielberg-like Backlight Wonder Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Wonder, encounter, childhood perspective, alien contact, miracle, suburban fantasy
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, franchise creatures, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Spielberg's Encounter light: a figure silhouetted in a doorway against an impossibly bright backlight — the source is so bright it creates no detail in the subject, only a dark shape inside a field of white-gold light. The viewer leans toward the light. Child eye-line: the camera is at 90cm height, making adults' hips the top of the frame. Lens flare from the backlight source is not a mistake, it is the message: something divine has arrived.

refs.name: Wong Kar-wai-like Neon Melancholy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Memory, yearning, Hong Kong, slow cinema, missed connections, neon rain atmosphere
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, brand signage, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Wong Kar-wai's blurred yearning: a figure in motion through a neon-lit corridor or night market, shot at half-speed or with step-printing that breaks them into afterimage layers — multiple ghost positions of the same body moving through the same space, all slightly out of sync. Palette: saturated green-teal neon, red-amber street lamps, midnight blue. The frame is always about missing something — a person, a time, a city that no longer exists.

refs.name: Villeneuve-like Fog Monolith Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: First contact, impossible scale, sci-fi awe, fog/atmosphere as staging tool, slow revelation
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, alien/ship design copies, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Villeneuve's monolith arrival: an enormous object — alien ship, desert worm, industrial structure — emerges slowly from fog or sand at the bottom of the frame. Its scale only becomes clear when a tiny human figure is visible at the edge. The object moves at a speed too slow to register in real time. Palette: cold grey-blue fog, warm amber sand, the object is always dark (charcoal-black or deep brown-grey) against the pale diffused sky. Silence is mandatory.

refs.name: Malick-like Golden Poetry Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Spiritual introspection, guilt and grace, nature as divine, wide-open landscapes, poetic drama
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, voiceover imitation as content, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Malick's golden-hour internal monologue: a figure walking through a field of tall grass at magic hour, the camera circling at waist height — grass in the foreground in soft focus, figure intermittently visible, sky burning amber-gold behind. Voice-over would go here. No establishing shot, no exposition, no eye-lines that make sense geographically. The cinematography (Lubezki) makes the world feel remembered rather than present. It IS a memory, even if it hasn't happened yet.

refs.name: Hitchcock-like Suspense Geometry Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Suspense geometry, voyeurism, pursuit, architectural dread, The Wrong Man, MacGuffin logic
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, film-title references, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Hitchcock's geometric anxiety: a staircase seen from directly above — the spiral or zigzag descent visible as pure graphic form, a figure descending it reduced to a shape moving through a pattern. Or: a long hotel corridor from ground level, two parallel walls, the subject at the far end making the destination visible but far. The camera holds on the geometry after the subject has left, because the geometry is still the problem. Palette: crisp black-and-white or Technicolor green-amber.

refs.name: Del Toro-like Gothic Fairy Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Dark fairy tale, creature beauty, childhood trauma, Francoist Spain, golden labyrinth
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, creature design copies, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Del Toro's tactile fairy horror: extreme close-up on a creature or object with practical effects — organic texture, eyeballs in unexpected locations, bioluminescent patches on insect-like limbs, gold-leaf filigree on a monstrous surface. The monster is beautiful. Interior of the old house: warm amber practical light, deep shadow, books and insects and clocks. Pale child against the dark world. Palette: deep red, amber-gold, verdigris green, pale cream, absolute black.

refs.name: Tarantino-like Pulp Frame Grammar (IP-safe)
refs.use: Crime comedy-drama, pop-culture dialogue, non-linear narrative, explosive violence in ordinary frame
refs.avoid: director name-dropping, violence glorification in output, exact shot copy
refs.dna: Tarantino's static pop shot: the camera is at table-top or dashboard level, looking slightly upward at a figure who is doing something mundane (eating, talking, driving) while something about the context is wrong. Long pauses in cutting — faces held at duration. Dialogue is the action. When violence comes, it is quick and without preparation. Palette: 1970s warm film stock (golden-orange, avocado, washed-out blue), or high-contrast red-white-black for the chapters.

refs.name: Turkish Folk Art — Iznik and Anatolian
refs.use: Turkish/Anatolian cultural identity, folk art, geometric pattern, cultural education, heritage design
refs.avoid: Western realism, photorealism, non-geometric organic forms
refs.dna: Iznik tilework geometric border patterns in deep cobalt, turquoise, white, and Ottoman red, flat precise floral tulip and carnation motif geometry, symmetrical radial composition logic, warm terracotta ground tone, handcraft surface quality without digital smoothness, decorative border as structural frame element

refs.name: Soul Warmth
refs.use: Emotional warmth, jazz club intimacy, quiet human moments, urban tenderness, grief and joy
refs.dna: Pete Docter's Soul: amber jazz-club practicals at 3am, the piano bench and the quiet of New York before the city wakes. Skin rendered with subsurface warmth, faces modeled for emotional specificity — the slight asymmetry that makes a character real, not beautiful. Upper-frame negative space breathes. Palette: warm amber, dusty gold, soft periwinkle, the particular deep blue of NYC night. Emotional truth through restraint — the quiet held moment, not the big gesture. Tears come from the smallest things: the right chord, an empty chair, light through venetian blinds.

refs.name: Apple Commercial Grammar
refs.use: Product reveal, premium hardware, material craft, engineering excellence, minimalist brand storytelling
refs.dna: Apple's product theology (Jony Ive/Lee Clow era): a single device in absolute void — the background not white but the specific near-white that reads as light itself. One edge-light from above-left grazes the aluminum chassis, defining object geometry with jeweler's precision. The glass surface holds one perfect specular highlight traveling the curved surface. No shadow is accidental — each is designed. The frame holds near-silence. The product is not demonstrated; it is worshipped. The visual promise: the person who uses this object is the kind of person who notices what most people miss.

refs.name: Nike Energy
refs.use: Athletic achievement, motivational energy, physical peak, sport action, aspirational heroism
refs.dna: Wieden+Kennedy's Nike grammar: an athlete frozen at the single 1/1000th-second frame of maximum biomechanical commitment — highest point of a jump, full sprint extension, peak pivot torque — the frame that contains all the training that preceded it. Hard directional key from one side creates a motion-implying shadow streak across the ground. The background is irrelevant; the body is everything. Palette: darkness with one energy accent — Nike orange flash, electric green, court red. The silhouette reads as a logo. No corporate framing — pure athletic will, raw and singular.

refs.name: DreamWorks — Hero Comedy Grammar
refs.use: Adventure comedy, expressive heroism, buddy dynamics, epic world with comedic protagonist
refs.dna: DreamWorks adventure grammar (How to Train Your Dragon/Kung Fu Panda era): the hero in a pose exactly halfway between genuine courage and total self-satisfaction — one beat past confident, just tipping toward comedic. Warm adventure key from above-angle, stylized caricature proportions (big expressive hands, arched brow, medium-large eyes that can hold both heroism and panic). Ensemble comedy staging: side characters in reaction, reinforcing the hero's misplaced confidence. The world behind them — mountains, ocean, ancient city — is genuinely epic and they are charmingly, gloriously inadequate to it. Palette: warm amber adventure gold, sky cerulean, earthy greens.

refs.name: Illumination — Pop Comedy Grammar
refs.use: High-energy comedy, crowd scenes, simple readable forms, cheerful chaos, family comedy
refs.dna: Illumination's maximum-saturation grammar: primary-color boldness, zero shadow complexity, overhead ambient key that flattens depth into graphic readability. Round forms, big expressive shapes, instantly readable at any scale. The comedy lives in the gap between a character's total confidence in their terrible plan and the physics about to destroy it. Clean background zones, one bold focal accent per scene, chaotic but joyful crowd staging. The emotional register is cheerful chaos — not ironic, not melancholy, genuinely full of combustible fun. The perfect pop illustration brought to life.

refs.name: Digimon — Digital Adventure Grammar
refs.use: Digital world texture, child adventure, creature partnership, wonder, crossing into another world
refs.dna: Toei's Digimon grammar (1999 season): the threshold between the real world and the Digital World — a glowing grid horizon, pixel-break texturing at object edges where digital physics begin to show, the warm orange of adventure-anime sunset sky behind digital terrain. Human child framed with partner creature, establishing scale (creature larger but protective). Bold cel-shading, clean outlines, optimistic palette: deep blue digital sky, warm orange adventure light, primary color boldness on both child and creature. The frame's promise: there is another world, and you belong in it.

refs.name: Football Stadium Energy Grammar
refs.use: Stadium scale, crowd atmosphere, broadcast sport, national pride, live event energy
refs.dna: The grammar of broadcast football: stadium corner shot — 70,000 humans as a color field, the green pitch a geometric precision in floodlight white below. Four flood-column beams rise into the night sky. Wide 24mm from just behind the goal line, the pitch converging toward midfield in perspective that emphasizes ritual scale. The athlete on the pitch is a tiny figure in a cathedral — sport as ancient theater. National team color palette in the crowd mass. The camera is absolutely still: this is not chaos, this is ceremony. The frame holds the collective breath of 70,000 people in absolute silence before the action begins.

refs.name: Arcane Zaun Dark
refs.use: Dark drama, undercity atmosphere, industrial gothic, corruption, mature storytelling
refs.avoid: Copying Vi/Jinx/Silco likenesses, actual League of Legends lore
refs.dna: Fortiche Studio's Zaun grammar: oil-paint brush strokes visible in the texture of every surface — painted realism, the opposite of photographic. The palette is poison: toxic green-yellow atmospheric haze from chem-tech processing, ember-orange warmth from chimney practicals, deep shadow masses consuming 70% of the frame. Architecture is industrial gothic — exposed pipes, industrial weight of corroded iron and copper, grated walkways, chemical steam rising. There is no overhead source here — illumination rises from below: glowing chemical pipes, ember fire, bioluminescent drainage. No surface is clean. The beauty is inseparable from the corruption.

refs.name: Spider-Verse Gwen Watercolor
refs.use: Creativity, music, self-expression, art, softer emotional content, dance, performance
refs.avoid: Gwen Stacy likeness, Spider-Woman costume, Marvel IP
refs.dna: Spider-Verse's Ghost-Spider visual grammar: watercolor wash fields bleeding at their edges, the background dissolving into soft pink-teal-white washes as if the scene is occurring inside a living illustration. Clean ink outlines hold the forms while the world behind them is pure emotion made visible. Slight chromatic offset registration — deliberate misalignment as aesthetic choice. Pastel radial spotlight on the subject, flat pop-art fields of color, negative space used freely. The visual logic: this is an image of a feeling, not a photograph of a place. Music, creativity, light movement, states of grace — things that can't be photographed, only painted.

refs.name: MHA Hero Panel Grammar
refs.use: Courage, heroism, effort, achievement, sports climax, decisive moment, peak determination
refs.avoid: Specific hero costumes (All Might, Deku, etc.), Quirk names, UA High School
refs.dna: My Hero Academia's shounen heroism grammar: low-angle up-shot against an open sky or light-blasted background, bold black-white speed lines radiating from the peak-effort point, the body at maximum extension, the expression at maximum determination. Cel-shading with strong rim light, clean linework at high contrast, comic-panel composition logic — tight crop, single decisive figure, the world beyond them a blur of motion or energy. Palette: blue sky, white power-release flash, warm amber hero-color accent. The visual declaration: all might is earned, nothing is given, and everything is possible if you give everything you have.

golden.name: IMAGE · Product Hero · Nano Banana
golden.bad: A cinematic shot of a premium phone case, dramatic lighting, ultra realistic, 4K.

golden.gold: Ultra-real premium commercial studio frame, exact product geometry locked, macro 100mm lens, clean black negative space, controlled gold rim light across aramid fiber texture, crisp edge highlights, realistic micro-scratches and weave depth, stable logo/packaging plane if present, shallow depth of field, product centered as hero object, no CGI plastic sheen, no warped text, no extra objects, frozen cinematic start frame ready for motion.

golden.name: IMAGE · Human Testimonial · Realism
golden.bad: A happy customer talking to camera in a nice office, cinematic.
golden.why_bad: Stock testimonial. No skin texture, eye-line, wardrobe fabric, practical light, human imperfection, location credibility.
golden.gold: Photoreal human portrait film frame, 50mm lens at eye height, natural skin texture with subtle pores and asymmetry, believable eye-line slightly off-camera, real wardrobe fabric with visible weave, soft practical key light from window left, muted office background with authentic depth, restrained color grade, human-scale framing, calm expression, no plastic skin, no fake smile, no beauty smoothing, frozen start frame.

golden.name: IMAGE · Education · Object-led
golden.bad: A Pixar classroom where kids learn about liquid measurements.
golden.why_bad: Too literal/classroom. No tactile metaphor, dominant lesson-object, motion hook.
golden.gold: Premium 3D Pixar-quality animated feature frame inside a handcrafted soft-clay learning world, one oversized transparent measuring beaker as the dominant lesson-object, warm classroom sunlight, tactile rounded materials, measurement marks physically engraved on a wooden base, liquid surface paused halfway with a visible light reflection ready to move, foreground clay droplets leading the eye, clean readable composition, no flat worksheet, no random labels, motion-ready start frame.

golden.name: MOTION · Product Hero · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: Camera zooms in while light sweeps over the phone case.
golden.why_bad: Lazy zoom. No parallax, no physical reflection choreography, no start/end discipline, no tail hold.
golden.gold: Low macro dolly slides once along the black tabletop, a blurred foreground edge giving parallax; as it arrives a single gold rim highlight travels once across the aramid weave and lands on the logo plane, which stays fixed and readable. Product geometry locked, no shape change. The move completes by ~5s into a three-quarter hero hold with clean negative space and a faint specular shimmer. Negative field: morphing, warping logo/text, changing product shape, extra objects, flicker. For more coverage add a second short beat instead of stretching this one.

golden.name: MOTION · Animation Education · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: The camera slowly pushes in and the objects glow.
golden.why_bad: Generic AI-video. No material/world physics, no educational reveal, no environment reaction, no final frame logic.
golden.gold: Child-eye push toward the clay lesson table; the liquid surface rises in ONE clean physical move and stops exactly at the engraved mark, a single warm highlight settling on the glass. Beaker geometry, table, engraved text and the characters stay fixed; one clay droplet wobbles once from the motion. Main event done by ~5s, then a stable three-quarter final hold with the level locked at the mark. Negative field: morphing, warping engraved text, changing faces, opening mouths, extra liquid, flicker. This beat completes well within Kling 3.0's 10s window — for more VO coverage, chain a second approved beat rather than stretching this one.

golden.name: MOTION · Documentary Civic · Kling 3.0
golden.bad: Drone shot of the city with cinematic music and people smiling.
golden.why_bad: Stock civic montage. No human scale, no motivated camera, no authentic public texture.
golden.gold: Handheld micro-drift begins at child height beside a real sidewalk, following the chalk line as pedestrians pass naturally in soft background parallax. The camera does not fly; it moves like a documentary operator walking slowly, letting practical morning light reveal the civic texture of paving stones, storefront glass and passing shadows. by the late beat the line leads into a modest public square where children’s movement creates the reveal. Final hold: stable human-scale frame, no politician hero shot, no stock crowd, no artificial glow.

golden.gold: Narration-safe instrumental documentary score, 82 BPM, soft felt piano pulse, restrained warm strings, low sub texture, subtle brushed percussion, no vocals, no lead melody during dialogue, gentle rise at midpoint, final warm resolve, clean mix with voice-over space, custom mode style layers: documentary, civic warmth, restrained cinematic, human scale.
